title: Make podman container list == podman ps
body:
Also make
podman create -p PUBLISHPorts
work.

This PR fixes ps_test.go

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
One nit.  The changes LGTM in general, but I'd like to get a head nod from @mheon as I'm not 100% sure on all the port processing removal.

@baude PTAL

@TomSweeneyRedHat @mheon This PR was about etting the ps test to pass and fixing one of the port handling issues (Just to get the test to pass.)  The Port handling needs more work and needs to be handled on the server side, and was not intended to be fixed here.  The changes you see on port handling here were around a function on the client side to validate user input.  This function was copied from old code and was basically doing little other then the validation.

We need to add Expose to Specgen, probably as either a `[]uint16` or `map[uint16]strct{}`, so we can handle it on the backend. Otherwise, I suppose this LGTM - we'll need the code here, in modified form, for the backend, but we can probably source from the v1.9 branch if we need to?
